run_metadata.notes: History lookup completed: audits/open_findings.json and audits/index.json contain no prior performance findings/runs for tracked categories.

agent.name: performance-cost-auditor
agent.role: Find performance bottlenecks, unnecessary costs, inefficient queries, oversized bundles, wasteful API calls, and rendering inefficiencies.
stop_conditions_hit: No runtime profiling/APM/Lighthouse data found; findings are static-analysis inferences unless marked evidence.
No executable bundle analyzer output available (bundle-stats artifact reports unknown --analyze option).
No query execution plans available; EXPLAIN ANALYZE requested as question findings.

findings.title: Conversation list computes unread counts with per-conversation count queries
findings.description: Conversation retrieval issues one base query then executes one `message.count` per conversation in a map. This creates an N+1 pattern and scales linearly with page size.

proof_hooks.summary: Per-conversation unread count query inside map
Expected batched group count vs observed fan-out counts
proof_hooks.expected: 1 query to fetch conversations + 1 grouped unread aggregation
proof_hooks.actual: 1 query to fetch conversations + N individual `message.count` calls

repro_steps: Call GET conversations endpoint with limit=20
Observe query count rising with limit
findings.impact: Higher DB round-trips and tail latency on inbox load; cost scales with active conversation count.

suggested_fix.approach: Replace per-row counts with grouped aggregation using `groupBy` on `conversationId` (or raw SQL CTE) and merge in memory.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Low risk; response shape unchanged if merged carefully.
tests_needed: Performance regression test for query count at limit=20/50
Functional test ensuring unread counts match previous behavior

findings.title: Unread total endpoint fans out count query for every conversation
findings.description: Unread totals are computed by loading all conversation IDs then invoking `getUnreadCountForConversation` for each conversation.

proof_hooks.summary: Conversation IDs loaded then per-ID unread count
Expected single grouped query vs observed N fan-out
proof_hooks.expected: Single grouped count query keyed by conversationId
proof_hooks.actual: 1 query for conversations + N count queries

findings.impact: Hot endpoint can become expensive for power users and drives unnecessary DB CPU.

suggested_fix.approach: Use one grouped count query with filters (`senderId != userId`, `status != READ`) and aggregate totals from grouped result.
tests_needed: Unit test comparing grouped totals to previous per-conversation logic

findings.title: Comment formatting performs per-comment like lookup
findings.description: Each comment calls `like.findUnique` in `formatComment`, creating N additional queries for paginated comment lists.

proof_hooks.summary: Map over comments calls formatter with async like lookup
Expected single prefetch of likes for visible comment IDs
proof_hooks.expected: 1 query for comments + 1 query for all likes by user on those comment IDs
proof_hooks.actual: 1 query for comments + N `findUnique` like checks

findings.impact: Comment pages degrade in latency with larger page sizes and high engagement.

suggested_fix.approach: Prefetch liked comment IDs using `where: { userId, commentId: { in: [...] } }` and compute `isLiked` from a Set.
tests_needed: Integration test ensuring `isLiked` correctness for mixed liked/unliked comments

findings.title: Stripe webhook lacks idempotency guard and can reprocess retried events
findings.description: Stripe webhook handler processes events directly without storing/validating `event.id`, unlike the Mux webhook which has explicit dedupe persistence.

proof_hooks.summary: Stripe handler switch executes without event dedupe check
Mux webhook uses processed-event guard

repro_steps: Replay same Stripe event payload/event.id twice
Observe handler side effects invoked twice
findings.impact: Duplicate payout/tip processing risk and inflated metered calls/notification writes.

suggested_fix.approach: Implement processed-event table check for Stripe events (same pattern as Mux), transactional insert+handle to guarantee idempotency.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Requires migration and careful transaction boundaries.
tests_needed: Webhook replay test asserting second delivery is no-op

findings.title: Stripe account status/details endpoints call Stripe on every request without cache
findings.description: `/stripe-connect/status` and `/stripe-connect/account` invoke `stripe.accounts.retrieve` each time. Stripe API is metered/rate-limited and these routes are likely polled by UI.

proof_hooks.summary: Direct Stripe retrieve calls in service methods
Controller routes expose status/details without cache headers

findings.impact: Higher Stripe bill and increased risk of 429 rate limiting under dashboard polling.

suggested_fix.approach: Add short-lived cache (e.g., 30-120s) keyed by account ID and invalidate on Stripe webhook `account.updated`.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Slight staleness acceptable for status UI.

findings.title: Multipart presigned URL generation is sequential per part
findings.description: S3 multipart URL generation loops with awaited signing per part, producing linear latency growth for large uploads.

proof_hooks.summary: Serial await in part signing loop
Expected bounded concurrency behavior
proof_hooks.expected: Parallel signing with capped concurrency (e.g., 5-10) for lower tail latency

findings.impact: Slow upload initialization for large files and increased request timeout risk.

suggested_fix.approach: Generate part URL promises with bounded concurrency and preserve part order in output.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Need to avoid overwhelming signer if unconstrained.
tests_needed: Unit test validating URL count/order and reduced elapsed time for high part counts

findings.title: Messaging hook reconnect risk from unstable callback references
findings.description: Caller passes inline callbacks to `useMessaging`; hook `connect` depends on those callbacks and auto-connect effect depends on `connect`, which can trigger reconnect churn on rerenders.

proof_hooks.summary: Inline callbacks in DMInbox options object
connect useCallback and auto-connect effect dependency chain

repro_steps: Render DMInbox and trigger state changes unrelated to connection
findings.impact: Unnecessary websocket reconnects, duplicate event setup, and avoidable API/socket load.

suggested_fix.approach: Stabilize callbacks with refs inside hook or memoize callbacks in caller with `useCallback`; isolate connection effect from volatile handlers.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Careful to avoid stale event handlers.
tests_needed: Hook test asserting single connection across rerenders

findings.title: Feed rerender pressure from unstable callbacks and non-memoized post cards
findings.description: `useFeed` action callbacks depend on `posts`, changing references frequently; `Feed` passes these into every `PostCard`, which is not memoized and also creates inline handlers.

proof_hooks.summary: Action callbacks close over posts array
Post list maps all PostCard children with changing handlers
PostCard includes inline click closures and local like state

findings.impact: Higher commit time and dropped frames on long feeds.

suggested_fix.approach: Use stable action dispatchers (state updater form), memoize `PostCard` (`React.memo`), and pass stable handler references.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Need to keep optimistic updates consistent.
tests_needed: React profiler benchmark comparing rerender counts before/after

findings.title: Search flows trigger redundant network calls due missing/duplicate debounce patterns
findings.description: Marketplace search refetches on every filter keystroke, music track search fetches on each query change, and user search is double-debounced (component + hook), increasing latency and waste.

proof_hooks.summary: Marketplace effect reloads on each input dependency change
Track search fetches immediately on query change
UserSearchBar and useUserSearch both debounce 300ms
Additional debounce in hook

findings.impact: Higher API volume and slower perceived search responsiveness.

suggested_fix.approach: Use one debounce layer only, add request cancellation, and trigger marketplace fetch on submit/settled debounce instead of every keypress.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Minimal behavior changes if thresholds are tuned.
tests_needed: Debounce behavioral tests and API-call count assertions per input sequence

findings.title: Missing dynamic imports and image optimization increase bundle and runtime cost
findings.description: No `next/dynamic` usage found in app source, modal-heavy components are statically imported, and raw `` is used across key pages/components instead of `next/image` optimization.

proof_hooks.summary: No dynamic import usage found
PostCreator statically imports MusicSelectorModal
Raw image tags used in hot components
Shared avatar uses raw img
Bundle analyzer preflight artifact unavailable

findings.impact: Larger JS and image payloads, slower LCP/TTI on constrained devices.

suggested_fix.approach: Adopt `next/dynamic` for optional modals/sections, migrate hot-path images to `next/image`, and fix analyzer command to collect concrete chunk sizes in CI.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: May require image domain allowlist and layout tuning.

findings.title: Group listing sorts by memberCount without explicit schema index
findings.description: Groups query orders by `memberCount desc`; schema indexes `Group.slug/type/category/createdAt/deletedAt` but not `memberCount`.

proof_hooks.summary: Order by memberCount in group discovery query
No Group.memberCount index in Prisma model

findings.impact: Potential sort cost/full scan as group table grows.

suggested_fix.approach: Add index on `memberCount` (possibly composite with `deletedAt` / `type` depending on query cardinality).
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Index write overhead; validate with EXPLAIN ANALYZE first.

findings.title: Need EXPLAIN ANALYZE for heavy social discovery raw SQL and trending hashtag scan
findings.description: Raw SQL in follow/discovery services and full 24h hashtag scans may require additional indexes or query rewrite, but execution plans are unavailable.

proof_hooks.summary: Raw SQL joins/NOT EXISTS in network suggestion query
Raw SQL joins/NOT EXISTS in mutual recommendation query
Trending hashtag query loads all recent hashtags for in-memory aggregation
Execution plan request
proof_hooks.command: Run EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, BUFFERS) for the two raw SQL recommendation queries and for equivalent SQL of trending hashtags window scan
proof_hooks.expected: Index scans on follow/block join keys, bounded sort cost, acceptable row estimates

findings.impact: Unknown until measured; risk of hidden full scans and sort spill at scale.

suggested_fix.approach: Capture query plans in staging with realistic cardinality; add/adjust composite indexes only where plans confirm bottlenecks.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Do not guess plans; collect evidence first.
tests_needed: Plan snapshots and latency benchmarks in CI/perf env

next_actions.action: Fix messaging unread-count N+1 paths with grouped aggregation
next_actions.rationale: Highest backend latency/cost win with low implementation risk.

next_actions.action: Add Stripe webhook idempotency and short cache for account status/details
next_actions.rationale: Reduces duplicate billing side effects and metered API usage.

next_actions.action: Stabilize messaging/feed callback references and memoize heavy post items
next_actions.rationale: Directly reduces frontend rerender and reconnect churn.

next_actions.action: Implement debounced/cancelable search and remove duplicate debounce layer
next_actions.rationale: Cuts unnecessary API traffic and improves perceived responsiveness.

next_actions.action: Enable real bundle profiling and run Lighthouse + EXPLAIN ANALYZE baseline
next_actions.rationale: Converts inference findings into measurable targets and validates index work.
